dish_name: Italian Meat Sauce with Country Ribs
description: Italian Meat Sauce with Country Ribs is a traditional Sunday sauce made with bone-in country-style pork ribs simmered in a rich tomato sauce, perfect for serving over pasta.
ingredients: [{"name": "olive oil", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "pork country ribs (bone-in)", "unit": "pounds", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "large sweet onion", "unit": "large", "amount": "0.5"}, {"name": "garlic cloves", "unit": "cloves", "amount": "6"}, {"name": "crushed tomatoes", "unit": "28-ounce cans", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "whole peeled tomatoes", "unit": "28-ounce can", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "salt", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "0.75"}, {"name": "red pepper flakes", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "0.75"}, {"name": "bay leaf", "unit": "leaf", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "fresh basil leaves", "unit": "leaves", "amount": "6-8"}, {"name": "pasta", "unit": "for serving", "amount": null}]
instructions: 1. Heat two tablespoons of olive oil in an extra-large skillet on high heat and brown the ribs on all sides for about 15 minutes. 2. Peel and chop half a large sweet onion. In a large pot, heat two tablespoons of olive oil on medium-high heat and add the onions. Stir occasionally. 3. Peel and chop garlic cloves and add to the pot when onions are nearly tender. 4. Transfer browned ribs and some skillet oil to the pot. Add crushed and whole peeled tomatoes, gently smashing whole tomatoes with a potato masher. 5. Stir in salt, red pepper flakes, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer gently with lid slightly ajar for at least 2.5 hours, stirring occasionally. 6. Cook until ribs are fork-tender, about 2 hours 45 minutes. 7. Boil salted pasta to serve with sauce. 8. Remove ribs with slotted spoon, discard loose bones, stir fresh basil into sauce, and adjust salt if needed. 9. Serve sauce over pasta and ribs as a second course or added to pasta. 10. Store leftovers refrigerated up to four days or freeze.
notes: Use bone-in country-style pork ribs for better flavor. Sear meat before adding to sauce. Slow cooker is not recommended as it causes overflow and runny sauce. Sauce can be used on pizza or with Italian bread. Leftovers can be refrigerated or frozen.
tags: Italian, Meat Sauce, Pork Ribs, Sunday Sauce, Main Course, Pasta Sauce
